description: Core prescribing cascade knowledge base for clinical decision support
disclaimer: For decision support only. Always verify with current clinical guidelines.

cascades.name_en: NSAID → Hypertension → Antihypertensive
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Prostaglandin inhibition → sodium/water retention + vasoconstriction → BP elevation
differential_hints: Essential hypertension poorly controlled prior to NSAID initiation
White coat hypertension (24h ambulatory monitoring may clarify)
High dietary sodium intake as primary driver
Obesity-related hypertension independent of NSAID use
Renal parenchymal disease causing secondary hypertension
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider if NSAIDs are truly necessary. In PLHIV, prefer paracetamol when possible.
references: Scott IA et al. Med J Aust 2013
Lanas A et al. Aliment Pharmacol Ther 2003

cascades.name_en: NSAID → GI Symptoms → PPI
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Gastric COX-1 inhibition → reduced protective mucus → GI irritation/ulceration
cascades.ade_en: Dyspepsia, peptic ulcer, nausea, abdominal pain
differential_hints: Helicobacter pylori infection as primary cause of dyspepsia
Functional dyspepsia unrelated to NSAID use
Dietary factors or alcohol contributing to GI symptoms
GERD pre-existing prior to NSAID initiation
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: If NSAID is stopped, the PPI may not be needed. In PLHIV verify PPI-ARV interactions.
references: Lanas A et al. 2006
Targownik LE et al. 2008

cascades.name_es: IECA → Tos → Antitusivo / Cambio a ARA-II
cascades.name_en: ACE Inhibitor → Cough → Antitussive / Switch to ARB
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Bradykinin accumulation → respiratory tract irritation → dry cough
differential_hints: Respiratory tract infection as cause of acute cough
Post-nasal drip syndrome unrelated to ACEi
Asthma or eosinophilic bronchitis independent of medication
Cough pre-existing prior to ACE inhibitor initiation
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: The correct solution is to switch ACE inhibitor to ARB, not add an antitussive.
references: Dicpinigaitis PV. Chest 2006

cascades.name_es: Antagonista del calcio → Edema maleolar → Diurético
cascades.name_en: Calcium Channel Blocker → Ankle Edema → Diuretic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Preferential arteriolar vasodilation → increased capillary pressure → peripheral edema
differential_hints: Heart failure as cause of ankle edema (require echocardiography)
Venous insufficiency or varicose veins as primary etiology
Deep vein thrombosis (unilateral edema requires exclusion)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider reducing CCB dose, switching to less vasodilatory dihydropyridine, or adding ACEi/ARB.
references: Elliott WJ. J Clin Hypertens 2003

cascades.name_en: Corticosteroid → Hyperglycemia → Antidiabetic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Glucocorticoid-induced insulin resistance → hyperglycemia
differential_hints: Type 2 diabetes mellitus newly diagnosed independent of corticosteroid
Stress hyperglycemia in acute illness context
Dietary non-adherence as primary driver of hyperglycemia
Other diabetogenic medications (e.g., tacrolimus, atypical antipsychotics)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Review whether corticosteroid can be reduced/discontinued. Insulin is often more appropriate than metformin for steroid-induced hyperglycemia.
references: Clore JN, Thurby-Hay L. Ann Pharmacother 2009

cascades.name_en: Corticosteroid → Osteoporosis → Bisphosphonate
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Osteoblast inhibition + osteoclast activation → bone loss
differential_hints: Primary osteoporosis (postmenopausal or age-related) independent of corticosteroid
Vitamin D deficiency as primary contributor to bone loss
Secondary osteoporosis from hypogonadism or thyroid disease
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: May be an appropriate prescription if corticosteroid is needed long-term. Evaluate the context.
references: Weinstein RS. Endocrinol Metab Clin North Am 2012

cascades.name_en: Thiazide → Hyperuricemia/Gout → Allopurinol/NSAID
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Reduced renal urate excretion → hyperuricemia → gout attack
differential_hints: Primary gout with genetic predisposition independent of diuretic
High purine diet as primary driver of hyperuricemia
Renal impairment reducing urate excretion independently
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: If thiazide can be stopped, urate levels may normalize. Evaluate indication.
references: Choi HK et al. BMJ 2012

cascades.name_en: Opioid → Constipation → Laxative
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Activation of mu-opioid receptors in enteric plexus → reduced peristalsis → constipation
differential_hints: Inadequate fluid or fibre intake as primary cause of constipation
Immobility-related constipation in palliative or post-surgical patients
Irritable bowel syndrome with constipation predominance
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: In this case the laxative may be appropriate. Evaluate if the opioid can be reduced or stopped.
references: Pappagallo M. Am J Surg 2001

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic → Extrapyramidal Symptoms → Anticholinergic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: D2 dopaminergic blockade in nigrostriatal pathway → extrapyramidal symptoms
differential_hints: Idiopathic Parkinson's disease unrelated to antipsychotic use
Essential tremor not caused by D2 blockade
Drug-induced akathisia versus agitation from underlying psychiatric illness
Tardive dyskinesia (late-onset, different mechanism from acute EPS)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Anticholinergics have many adverse effects themselves (confusion, urinary retention, glaucoma). Avoid in elderly.
references: Kane JM et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2009

cascades.name_en: SSRI → Insomnia → Hypnotic/Sedative
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonergic activation → central stimulation → insomnia, especially at initiation
differential_hints: Insomnia as symptom of underlying depression not yet responding to SSRI
Anxiety disorder causing sleep disturbance independently
Poor sleep hygiene as primary contributor
Circadian rhythm disruption from shift work or jet lag
Substance use (caffeine, alcohol) interfering with sleep
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Hypnotics carry dependency risk. Consider sleep hygiene, changing SSRI timing, or switching antidepressant.
references: Fava M et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2006

cascades.name_es: Inhibidor de colinesterasa → Incontinencia urinaria → Anticolinérgico
cascades.name_en: Cholinesterase Inhibitor → Urinary Incontinence → Anticholinergic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Bladder cholinergic excess → involuntary detrusor contractions → urge incontinence
differential_hints: Overactive bladder syndrome unrelated to cholinesterase inhibitor
Urinary tract infection causing urgency and incontinence
Pelvic floor weakness in older women (stress incontinence)
Benign prostatic hyperplasia causing overflow incontinence in men
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Particularly problematic cascade: treating incontinence reduces effectiveness of Alzheimer's treatment.
references: Gill SS et al. Arch Intern Med 2005

cascades.name_en: Metformin → GI Symptoms → Antidiarrheal/Antispasmodic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: GI microbiota alteration and motility changes → diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain
differential_hints: Irritable bowel syndrome as primary cause of GI symptoms
Infectious gastroenteritis coinciding with metformin initiation
Lactose intolerance or dietary factors driving GI complaints
Celiac disease or inflammatory bowel disease as alternative diagnosis
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Preferred solution: extended-release formulation or dose reduction, not adding an antidiarrheal.
references: Bailey CJ et al. Lancet 1996

cascades.name_en: Anticholinergic → Constipation → Laxative
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Muscarinic blockade in enteric nervous system → reduced GI motility → constipation
differential_hints: Inadequate dietary fibre or fluid intake
Immobility in older or frail patients
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Review anticholinergic burden (ACB score). Reduce or switch to agent with lower anticholinergic load rather than adding laxative.
references: Rochon PA, Gurwitz JH. Lancet 1995
Brath H et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol 2019

cascades.name_en: Anticholinergic → Dry Mouth → Saliva Substitute / Pilocarpine
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Muscarinic M3 receptor blockade in salivary glands → reduced saliva production → xerostomia
differential_hints: Sjögren's syndrome or other autoimmune salivary gland disease
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Reassess anticholinergic burden. Switch to agent with lower anticholinergic load (e.g., mirabegron for OAB) rather than adding sialagogue.
references: Durán CE et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol 2013

cascades.name_en: Opioid → Nausea/Vomiting → Antiemetic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Stimulation of chemoreceptor trigger zone (area postrema) and vestibular system by opioids → nausea and vomiting
differential_hints: Nausea from underlying disease (oncology, renal failure) not opioid-related
Vestibular cause of nausea (opioid-induced) vs labyrinthine disease
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Antiemetic is often appropriate short-term. Reassess at 2 weeks — opioid-induced nausea typically improves with tolerance. Avoid promethazine in elderly.
references: Benyamin R et al. Pain Physician 2008

cascades.name_en: Opioid → Sedation → Stimulant / Opioid Dose Reduction
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: CNS mu-opioid receptor activation → respiratory depression and sedation
differential_hints: Depression-related fatigue and psychomotor slowing independent of opioid
Anaemia causing fatigue (check Hb in PLHIV)
HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder contributing to drowsiness
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider opioid dose reduction, rotation, or switching to a less sedating analgesic before adding a stimulant.
references: Slatkin NE. J Support Oncol 2009

cascades.name_en: Beta-Blocker → Fatigue/Exercise Intolerance → Stimulant
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Beta-1 blockade → reduced cardiac output + skeletal muscle vasoconstriction → fatigue and exercise intolerance
differential_hints: Heart failure itself as primary cause of fatigue (not beta-blocker)
Anaemia or hypothyroidism causing fatigue independently
HIV-associated fatigue syndrome unrelated to beta-blocker
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Assess whether beta-blocker dose can be reduced or switched to cardioselective agent. Exercise rehabilitation may be more appropriate.
references: Brath H et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol 2019

cascades.name_en: Beta-Blocker → Depression → Antidepressant
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: CNS beta-blockade + possible interference with serotonin and noradrenaline pathways → depressive symptoms
differential_hints: Major depressive disorder pre-existing or independent of beta-blocker
Cardiac disease burden itself causing reactive depression
Chronic pain contributing to depressed mood
HIV-related psychosocial stressors as primary driver
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Evaluate temporal relationship to beta-blocker initiation. Consider switching to hydrophilic beta-blocker (atenolol) with less CNS penetration before adding antidepressant.
references: Ko DT et al. JAMA 2002

cascades.name_en: SSRI / SNRI → Sexual Dysfunction → PDE5 Inhibitor
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonergic excess → 5-HT2 receptor activation + prolactin elevation → inhibition of nitric oxide release → sexual dysfunction
cascades.ade_en: Sexual dysfunction (delayed ejaculation, anorgasmia, decreased libido, erectile dysfunction)
differential_hints: Sexual dysfunction as symptom of underlying depression itself
Testosterone deficiency (common in PLHIV) as primary driver
Vascular erectile dysfunction independent of antidepressant
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: PDE5 inhibitors + ritonavir-boosted regimens: significant increase in PDE5 inhibitor levels. Start sildenafil at 25 mg max; tadalafil 10 mg no more than q72h.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Discuss dose reduction or switch to antidepressant with lower sexual side-effect burden (bupropion, mirtazapine) before adding PDE5 inhibitor. Check testosterone level.
references: Clayton AH et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2002
DHHS Guidelines 2024

cascades.name_en: Diuretic (Loop/Thiazide) → Hypokalemia → Potassium Supplement
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Increased urinary potassium excretion via sodium-potassium exchange in distal tubule → hypokalaemia
differential_hints: GI losses (diarrhoea/vomiting) as primary cause of hypokalaemia
Renal tubular acidosis in TDF-treated patients (VIH002)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Potassium supplementation is often appropriate. Consider switching to potassium-sparing diuretic (spironolactone) or ACEi/ARB if clinically appropriate.
references: Alfonzo AV et al. Nephrol Dial Transplant 2006

cascades.name_en: Diuretic → Dehydration / Orthostatic Hypotension → Falls / Electrolyte Replacement
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Excessive volume depletion → reduced cerebral perfusion on standing → orthostatic hypotension → falls
differential_hints: Pre-existing autonomic neuropathy (HIV, diabetes) contributing to orthostatic hypotension
Cardiac cause of syncope requiring investigation
Dehydration from concurrent illness rather than diuretic alone
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Reassess diuretic dose and indication. Orthostatic BP measurement recommended. Consider dose reduction or switch before adding bone protection.
references: Dhruv Kazi et al. JAMA Intern Med 2016

cascades.name_en: Antidepressant (TCA/SNRI) → Urinary Retention → Alpha-Blocker / Catheter
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Anticholinergic + alpha-1 adrenergic blockade → relaxation of detrusor + tightening of bladder neck → urinary retention
differential_hints: Benign prostatic hyperplasia as primary cause in men
Neurogenic bladder from HIV neuropathy or diabetes
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Switch antidepressant to agent with lower anticholinergic burden (SSRI, mirtazapine). Treat prostatic disease definitively if confirmed.
references: Brath H et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol 2019

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic → Hyperprolactinaemia → Dopamine Agonist
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: D2 receptor blockade in tuberoinfundibular pathway → removal of dopamine inhibition → elevated prolactin
differential_hints: Renal failure causing reduced prolactin clearance
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Switch to prolactin-sparing antipsychotic (quetiapine, aripiprazole, clozapine) rather than adding a dopamine agonist.
references: Meaney AM, O'Keane V. Int J Neuropsychopharmacol 2002

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic / Atypical → Weight Gain / Metabolic Syndrome → Antidiabetic / Lipid-lowering
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: H1, 5-HT2C, dopamine D2 antagonism → appetite stimulation + insulin resistance → weight gain and metabolic syndrome
cascades.ade_en: Weight gain, type 2 diabetes, dyslipidaemia, metabolic syndrome
differential_hints: Pre-existing type 2 diabetes or dyslipidaemia before antipsychotic
INSTI-associated weight gain in HIV-positive patients on antipsychotics
Sedentary lifestyle and caloric excess as primary driver
Corticosteroid co-administration contributing to metabolic effects
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider switching to metabolically neutral antipsychotic (aripiprazole, ziprasidone) before adding cardiometabolic drugs. Monitor BMI, glucose and lipids regularly.
references: Newcomer JW. J Clin Psychiatry 2004

cascades.name_en: Loop Diuretic → Hypomagnesaemia → Magnesium Supplement
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Blockade of NKCC2 transporter in thick ascending loop → increased urinary magnesium wasting → hypomagnesaemia
cascades.ade_en: Hypomagnesaemia, muscle cramps, cardiac arrhythmia risk
differential_hints: Dietary magnesium deficiency as primary cause
PPI-induced magnesium malabsorption (particularly with long-term PPI use)
Alcohol misuse causing renal magnesium wasting
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Magnesium supplementation is appropriate. Assess concurrent PPI use (double diuretic + PPI hypomagnesaemia risk). Consider dose reduction of loop diuretic if feasible.
references: Ryan MP. Annu Rev Pharmacol Toxicol 1984

cascades.name_en: PPI (long-term) → Hypomagnesaemia → Magnesium Supplement
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Impaired TRPM6/7 channel-mediated magnesium absorption in intestine due to altered luminal pH → hypomagnesaemia
cascades.ade_en: Hypomagnesaemia (after ≥3 months of PPI therapy), tetany, arrhythmia
differential_hints: Loop diuretic co-administration increasing magnesium wasting (synergistic)
Celiac disease or IBD causing malabsorption
Alcohol misuse contributing to renal wasting
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider whether PPI indication remains valid. H2 antagonist may be sufficient with lower hypomagnesaemia risk. If PPI required, monitor Mg and supplement as needed.
references: Cundy T, Mackay J. Aliment Pharmacol Ther 2011

cascades.name_en: Gabapentinoid → Peripheral Oedema → Diuretic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Alpha-2-delta calcium channel subunit blockade → peripheral vasodilation + fluid redistribution → limb oedema
differential_hints: CCB-induced oedema if patient on both CCB and gabapentinoid (CC004)
Cardiac or venous insufficiency as primary etiology
Hypoalbuminemia from nutritional deficiency common in PLHIV
Lymphedema from prior infection or malignancy
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Reassess indication for gabapentinoid. Dose reduction or switch to alternative analgesic/neuropathic pain agent is preferable to adding a diuretic.
references: Shneker BF, McAuley JW. Ann Pharmacother 2005

cascades.name_en: Bisphosphonate → Upper GI Symptoms → PPI
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Direct mucosal contact + altered epithelial tight junctions → oesophageal and gastric irritation
differential_hints: Incorrect bisphosphonate administration technique (not remaining upright 30 min)
H. pylori infection as independent cause of dyspepsia
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Verify correct administration technique first (full glass water, remain upright 30 min). Switch to IV bisphosphonate or quarterly ibandronate if oral intolerance confirmed. PPI adds drug burden unnecessarily if technique corrected.
references: Rosen CJ. N Engl J Med 2005

cascades.name_en: Antihypertensive (Multi-drug) → Erectile Dysfunction → PDE5 Inhibitor
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Beta-1 blockade reducing cardiac output + penile vasoconstriction; thiazide-induced zinc depletion reducing testosterone → erectile dysfunction
differential_hints: Testosterone deficiency (prevalent in PLHIV, aging men)
Vascular atherosclerosis as primary driver of erectile dysfunction
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: PDE5 inhibitors contraindicated with nitrates (severe hypotension). In PLHIV on PI/r, dose adjustment required — max sildenafil 25 mg/48h.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Check testosterone, assess cardiovascular risk, and consider switching antihypertensive to agent with less ED risk (ACEi, ARB, CCB) before prescribing PDE5 inhibitor.
references: Baumhäkel M et al. Int J Clin Pract 2011

cascades.name_en: SSRI → Hyponatraemia → Sodium Supplement / Fluid Restriction Review
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: SIADH via enhanced ADH secretion and renal V2 receptor potentiation → dilutional hyponatraemia
cascades.ade_en: Hyponatraemia (particularly in elderly), confusion, falls, seizures in severe cases
cascades.cascade_drug_class: Sodium supplement / Fluid restriction advice / Demeclocycline
differential_hints: Hypothyroidism or adrenal insufficiency as primary SIADH cause
Heart failure, cirrhosis, or nephrotic syndrome causing dilutional hyponatraemia
CNS disease causing SIADH in PLHIV (CNS infection, lymphoma)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: In most cases, stop or reduce the SSRI. Switching antidepressant class is preferable to adding sodium replacement or demeclocycline. Monitor sodium weekly at initiation in elderly.
references: Fabian TJ et al. J Clin Psychiatry 2004

cascades.name_en: Tricyclic Antidepressant → Orthostatic Hypotension → Fall-related Injury Treatment
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Alpha-1 adrenergic blockade → peripheral vasodilation → postural blood pressure drop → orthostatic hypotension → falls
differential_hints: Autonomic neuropathy from diabetes or HIV as independent contributor
Volume depletion from diuretic or inadequate fluid intake
Multiple antihypertensive polypharmacy causing cumulative hypotension
Primary osteoporosis in postmenopausal women or PLHIV on TDF
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Switch TCA to SSRI with lower orthostatic risk (sertraline, escitalopram) rather than treating fall consequences. Assess bone density in high-risk patients.
references: Woolcott JC et al. Arch Intern Med 2009

cascades.name_en: Corticosteroid → Mood Disturbance / Insomnia → Hypnotic / Antidepressant
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Glucocorticoid receptor activation in limbic system + HPA axis disruption → mood instability, hypomania, depression, insomnia
differential_hints: Pre-existing bipolar disorder or major depressive disorder
Underlying disease (malignancy, autoimmune) causing psychiatric symptoms
HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder contributing to mood instability
Steroid dose — mood disturbance dose-related (higher risk >20 mg prednisolone)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Taper corticosteroid to lowest effective dose. Avoid benzodiazepines and Z-drugs when possible. Consider quetiapine low-dose for sleep in steroid-induced insomnia with mood component.
references: Brown ES et al. Psychosom Med 2004

cascades.name_en: Statin → Myalgia / Muscle Weakness → Analgesic / Physiotherapy Referral
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Decreased CoQ10 synthesis + impaired mitochondrial function in myocytes → myopathy ranging from myalgia to rhabdomyolysis
cascades.ade_en: Myalgia, muscle weakness, elevated CK, myopathy
differential_hints: Drug interaction increasing statin exposure (PI/r inhibiting CYP3A4, CC001-related)
HIV-related myopathy or AZT (zidovudine) mitochondrial myopathy
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: PI/r dramatically increases simvastatin/lovastatin exposure. Prefer rosuvastatin, pravastatin, or pitavastatin in PLHIV on PI/r.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Stop statin and reassess after 4-6 weeks. If myalgia resolves, confirm statin causation. Switch to low-dose hydrophilic statin (pravastatin, rosuvastatin) rather than adding analgesic long-term.
references: Bruckert E et al. Cardiovasc Drugs Ther 2005

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic → QTc Prolongation → Anti-arrhythmic / ECG Monitoring Burden
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: hERG potassium channel blockade in cardiac myocytes → prolonged QT interval → increased risk of torsades de pointes
cascades.ade_en: QTc prolongation, increased arrhythmia risk (torsades de pointes)
differential_hints: Congenital long QT syndrome as independent risk factor
Concurrent medications also prolonging QT (fluoroquinolones, macrolides, antiemetics)
Electrolyte disturbances (hypokalaemia, hypomagnesaemia) amplifying QTc effect
Structural cardiac disease independently prolonging QT
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: Multiple QT-prolonging drugs are common in PLHIV (ARVs + antiemetics + antipsychotics). Use CredibleMeds or similar QT risk checker.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Correct electrolytes (K, Mg). Switch to antipsychotic with lower QTc risk (aripiprazole). Avoid combining with other QT-prolonging agents. Do not add amiodarone without specialist review.
references: Glassman AH, Bigger JT Jr. Am J Psychiatry 2001

cascades.name_en: Alpha-Blocker (for BPH) → Orthostatic Hypotension → Dizziness Medication
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Alpha-1 adrenergic receptor blockade → systemic vasodilation → orthostatic hypotension, especially when co-prescribed with antihypertensives
differential_hints: PDE5 inhibitor co-administration causing profound hypotension
Volume depletion from diuretic or poor fluid intake
Autonomic neuropathy from HIV or diabetes
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: Alpha-blocker + PDE5 inhibitor → severe hypotension. Use tamsulosin 0.4 mg if PDE5 inhibitor required; avoid first-line non-selective alpha-blockers.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Switch to uroselective tamsulosin if using non-selective agent. Review and reduce antihypertensive burden. Antihistamines for dizziness add sedation risk — address root cause instead.
references: Chapple CR. Eur Urol 2004

cascades.name_en: Progestogen-only / Combined Hormonal Contraceptive → Depression → Antidepressant
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Progestogen-mediated effects on neuroactive steroids and serotonin metabolism → mood changes, depressive symptoms
differential_hints: HIV-related stigma and psychosocial burden as primary driver
Vitamin D deficiency in women with limited sun exposure
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Explore temporal relationship. Consider switching to non-hormonal or progestogen-free contraception before initiating antidepressant. Note ARV-hormonal interactions (enzyme-inducing ARVs reduce contraceptive efficacy).
references: Skovlund CW et al. JAMA Psychiatry 2016

cascades.name_en: Anticholinergic (Bladder) → Cognitive Impairment / Delirium → Antipsychotic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Muscarinic M1 receptor blockade in prefrontal cortex and hippocampus → impaired cholinergic neurotransmission → cognitive impairment, confusion, delirium
differential_hints: Underlying dementia (HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder, Alzheimer's) worsening independently
CNS opportunistic infection in PLHIV causing acute confusion
Urinary tract infection as precipitant of delirium in frail elderly
Electrolyte disturbance (hyponatraemia — CC030) contributing to confusion
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Stop the offending anticholinergic. Switch to mirabegron (beta-3 agonist, no CNS anticholinergic effect) for OAB rather than adding antipsychotic. Antipsychotics worsen anticholinergic burden.
references: Gray SL et al. JAMA Intern Med 2015

cascades.name_en: Tramadol → Serotonin Toxicity / Seizures → Antiepileptic
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonin and noradrenaline reuptake inhibition + opioid activity → serotonin excess or lowered seizure threshold, especially with SSRI co-prescription
differential_hints: Serotonin toxicity from SSRI + tramadol combination (must assess concurrent SSRIs)
Epilepsy newly diagnosed independent of tramadol
HIV CNS disease lowering seizure threshold independently
Drug interaction: tramadol metabolism affected by CYP2D6 inhibitors (fluoxetine, paroxetine)
cascades.ddi_warning_en: CAUTION: Tramadol + SSRI/SNRI → significant serotonin toxicity risk. Tramadol + carbamazepine → reduced analgesia (CYP3A4 induction). Tramadol is generally avoided in patients on SSRIs.
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Discontinue tramadol and switch to a non-serotonergic opioid analgesic. Adding an antiepileptic without addressing the root cause is inappropriate.
references: Tashakkori M et al. Ann Emerg Med 2009

cascades.name_en: Long-term PPI → Osteoporosis / Fracture Risk → Bisphosphonate / Calcium Supplement
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Achlorhydria → reduced calcium and vitamin B12 absorption → secondary hyperparathyroidism + bone resorption; also reduced magnesium (CC026)
cascades.ade_en: Reduced bone density, increased hip/vertebral fracture risk with prolonged use
differential_hints: Age-related osteoporosis in postmenopausal women or older men
TDF-related bone loss in PLHIV (synergistic with PPI) — see VIH002
Vitamin D deficiency as primary contributor
Corticosteroid use as additional bone loss driver (CC006)
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Reassess PPI indication — consider step-down to lowest effective dose or switch to H2 antagonist. If PPI required long-term, ensure adequate calcium/vitamin D intake and consider DEXA scan.
references: Yang YX et al. JAMA 2006
Corley DA et al. Arch Intern Med 2010

cascades.name_en: Antidepressant (TCA/SNRI) → Weight Gain → Antidiabetic / Lipid-lowering
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: H1 antihistamine blockade + enhanced appetite via serotonin/noradrenaline effects → weight gain → metabolic consequences
cascades.ade_en: Weight gain, impaired glucose tolerance, new-onset type 2 diabetes
differential_hints: INSTI-associated weight gain in PLHIV contributing synergistically (VIH003)
Corticosteroid co-administration as additional metabolic driver
Pre-existing insulin resistance or family history of type 2 diabetes
Sedentary lifestyle and caloric excess as primary contributors
cascades.recommended_first_action_en: Consider switching to weight-neutral or weight-reducing antidepressant (bupropion, fluoxetine) before adding antidiabetic or lipid-lowering agent. Monitor weight, glucose and lipids at 3 and 6 months.
references: Serretti A, Mandelli L. J Clin Psychiatry 2010

cascades.name_en: Dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker-induced peripheral edema treated with diuretics
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Edema is caused by precapillary arteriolar dilation without compensatory venous return enhancement; it is not volume-overload. Diuretics do not address the mechanism, may worsen neurohormonal activation, and increase electrolyte disturbance risk. Preferred intervention is CCB dose reduction or substitution with a renin-angiotensin system agent.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Dilatación arteriolar precapilar sin compensación del retorno venoso → acumulación de líquido intersticial (no sobrecarga de volumen); los diuréticos no actúan sobre el mecanismo y pueden empeorar la activación neurohormonal; preferir reducción de dosis del BCC o sustitución por un agente del SRAA

cascades.name_en: ACE inhibitor-induced cough treated with antitussives
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: ACE inhibitor cough is caused by bradykinin and substance P accumulation due to inhibited angiotensin-converting enzyme degradation; antitussives are pharmacologically ineffective as definitive treatment and add adverse effects (sedation, constipation with codeine). The only definitive intervention is substitution with an ARB, which does not affect bradykinin metabolism.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Acumulación de bradicinina y sustancia P por inhibición de la enzima convertidora → tos seca crónica; los antitusivos son farmacológicamente ineficaces como tratamiento definitivo; la única intervención definitiva es la sustitución por un ARA-II

cascades.name_en: Beta-blocker-induced depression treated with antidepressants
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Evidence for beta-blocker-induced depression is weak and contested in placebo-controlled RCTs; confounding by underlying cardiac disease is substantial. Evidence is marginally stronger for lipophilic agents that cross the blood-brain barrier (propranolol > metoprolol). Atenolol has been excluded from the trigger list: it is hydrophilic (LogP -0.23), has negligible CNS penetration, and lacks credible evidence for depression induction. Adding an antidepressant without reviewing beta-blocker lipophilicity and indication increases polypharmacy burden. Switch to a hydrophilic beta-blocker (atenolol, bisoprolol) should be considered before adding an antidepressant.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Betabloqueo central (agentes lipófilos: propranolol > metoprolol) con posible interferencia en vías serotoninérgicas/noradrenérgicas; evidencia débil en ECAs controlados con placebo; confusión importante con cardiopatía subyacente; el atenolol se excluye del desencadenante por su perfil hidrofílico y penetración CNS negligible

cascades.name_es: Betabloqueante → Disfunción eréctil → Inhibidor de PDE5
cascades.name_en: Beta-blocker-induced erectile dysfunction treated with PDE5 inhibitors
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Beta-blockers reduce cardiac output and impair vasodilatory response in penile vasculature. PDE5 inhibitors cause additional vasodilation and hypotension, particularly dangerous in patients also on organic nitrates (absolute contraindication) or other antihypertensives. This cascade is frequent in cardiovascular patients and warrants structured medication review before PDE5 inhibitor initiation.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Reducción del gasto cardíaco y deterioro de la respuesta vasodilatadora en la vasculatura peneana; los inhibidores de PDE5 causan hipotensión adicional, especialmente con nitratos orgánicos (contraindicación absoluta) o con otros antihipertensivos; revisar indicación del betabloqueante antes de iniciar el inhibidor de PDE5

cascades.name_en: Thiazide diuretic-induced hyperuricemia treated with urate-lowering agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Thiazides and their metabolites compete at URAT1 (SLC22A12, the primary urate reabsorber) and OAT4 (SLC22A11) in the proximal tubule, reducing net renal urate excretion. Adding urate-lowering therapy without reviewing the thiazide is a recognized cascade. Losartan has uricosuric properties via URAT1 inhibition and may be a more rational antihypertensive choice in patients with concurrent gout or hyperuricemia. Indomethacin for acute gout in this population adds renal and cardiovascular risk and should be avoided.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Competencia con el urato en URAT1 (SLC22A12) y OAT4 (SLC22A11) en el túbulo proximal → reducción neta de la excreción renal de urato; el losartán tiene propiedades uricosúricas vía inhibición de URAT1 y puede ser la alternativa antihipertensiva más racional en pacientes con hiperuricemia o gota concomitante

cascades.name_en: Thiazide diuretic-induced hypokalemia treated with potassium supplements
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Adding potassium supplements or potassium-sparing diuretics without reviewing thiazide necessity is a recognized cascade. In patients with concurrent RAAS inhibition (ACE inhibitor, ARB, spironolactone), added potassium creates significant hyperkalemia risk. Concurrent hypomagnesemia (common with loop diuretics) prevents potassium repletion and must be corrected simultaneously.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Aumento del intercambio sodio-potasio en el túbulo distal → hipopotasemia; añadir suplementos de potasio sin revisar la necesidad de la tiazida es una cascada reconocida; en pacientes con inhibidores del SRAA concomitantes, el potasio añadido crea riesgo significativo de hiperpotasemia

cascades.name_en: Thiazide diuretic-induced hyperglycemia treated with antidiabetic agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Thiazides impair insulin secretion via hypokalemia-mediated KATP channel hyperpolarization in pancreatic beta-cells and increase peripheral insulin resistance. Diabetogenic risk varies within the class: chlorthalidone carries the highest risk, hydrochlorothiazide intermediate, and indapamide (predominantly a vasodilator at low doses, 1.5 mg SR) substantially lower metabolic risk. Including indapamide as an equal trigger may inflate false-positive alerts. Adding antidiabetic therapy without reconsidering the thiazide and switching to a metabolically neutral antihypertensive (ACE inhibitor, ARB, DHP-CCB) or to indapamide adds metabolic complexity and pill burden unnecessarily.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Hiperpolarización de células beta pancreáticas inducida por hipopotasemia → deterioro de la secreción de insulina + aumento de la resistencia insulínica periférica; riesgo diabetogénico variable: clortalidona > hidroclorotiazida > indapamida; añadir antidiabéticos sin considerar el cambio a antihipertensivo metabólicamente neutro incrementa la carga farmacológica

cascades.name_en: Amiodarone-induced hypothyroidism treated with levothyroxine
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Amiodarone contains approximately 37% iodine by weight. Amiodarone-induced hypothyroidism (AIH) results primarily from two distinct mechanisms: (1) the Wolff-Chaikoff effect — excess iodine load inhibits thyroid organification in iodine-replete individuals, impairing T3/T4 synthesis; (2) direct cytotoxic or immune-mediated thyroid damage from amiodarone and its active metabolite desethylamiodarone. Note: type 1 deiodinase inhibition by amiodarone causes T4→T3 conversion impairment and produces elevated T4 with low T3 (low T3 syndrome), which is a separate phenomenon from overt hypothyroidism and should not be conflated with AIH. Amiodarone has a half-life of approximately 40-100 days; thyroid dysfunction may persist for months after discontinuation. Adding levothyroxine without reassessing amiodarone indication is a recognized cascade, particularly in elderly patients.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Sobrecarga de yodo (≈37% del peso molecular) → efecto Wolff-Chaikoff + daño tiroideo citotóxico o inmunomediado por la amiodarona y su metabolito desethylamiodarona; semivida ~40-100 días; la disfunción tiroidea puede persistir meses tras la retirada; añadir levotiroxina sin reevaluar la indicación de amiodarona es una cascada reconocida

cascades.name_en: Alpha-1 adrenergic blocker-induced orthostatic hypotension treated with vasopressors
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Non-selective alpha-1 blockers (doxazosin, terazosin, prazosin) reduce sympathetic vascular tone via alpha-1A and alpha-1B blockade, causing venous pooling and orthostatic hypotension — high risk. Uro-selective alpha-1A antagonists (tamsulosin, silodosin) have substantially lower risk of systemic orthostatic hypotension due to high urothelial selectivity; alert weight should be differentiated in CDS implementation. Adding midodrine or fludrocortisone without reviewing alpha-blocker indication (BPH vs. hypertension), agent selection, and dose substantially increases cardiovascular burden and supine hypertension risk in elderly patients. Reviewing the indication, switching to a uro-selective agent, or dose reduction should precede vasopressor addition.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Bloqueo alfa-1A y alfa-1B no selectivo (doxazosina, terazosina, prazosina) → agrupamiento venoso e hipotensión postural; los alfa-1A uroselectives (tamsulosina, silodosina) tienen riesgo sistémico sustancialmente menor; añadir midodrina o fludrocortisona sin revisar el agente y la indicación aumenta considerablemente la carga cardiovascular

cascades.name_en: Statin-induced myalgia treated with analgesics or NSAIDs
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Statin-induced muscle symptoms affect 5-10% of patients, mediated by CoQ10 depletion and mitochondrial dysfunction. NSAIDs add GI, cardiovascular and renal risk without addressing the cause. CK should be measured; significant elevation warrants statin discontinuation. Switching to a hydrophilic statin (rosuvastatin, pravastatin) or alternate-day dosing often resolves symptoms without requiring analgesics.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Depleción de CoQ10 y disfunción mitocondrial en miocitos → miopatía (del 5-10% de pacientes); los AINE no actúan sobre el mecanismo y añaden riesgo GI, cardiovascular y renal; medir CK; cambiar a estatina hidrofílica (rosuvastatina, pravastatina) o a dosis alternas suele resolver los síntomas sin necesidad de analgésicos

cascades.name_en: SSRI/SNRI-induced SIADH treated with hyponatremia correction agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: SSRI/SNRI-induced SIADH is underrecognized and prevalent in elderly populations. Incidence of any hyponatremia (Na <135 mEq/L) in unselected elderly outpatients is approximately 10-15%; clinically significant hyponatremia (Na <130 mEq/L) occurs in approximately 1-4% of SSRI users overall, rising to 20-30% only in highly selected high-risk groups (elderly women, concurrent thiazide, low baseline sodium, low BMI). Initiating vasopressin receptor antagonists or sodium supplementation without identifying and withdrawing the causative SSRI/SNRI delays appropriate management. Sodium must be corrected at a safe rate (<10 mEq/L per 24h) to prevent osmotic demyelination syndrome.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Potenciación de la secreción de ADH y de los receptores V2 renales → hiponatremia dilucional; iniciar antagonistas del receptor de vasopresina o suplementos de sodio sin identificar y retirar el ISRS/IRSN causal retrasa el manejo adecuado; la corrección debe ser ≤10 mEq/L por 24 h para evitar el síndrome de desmielinización osmótica

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic-induced extrapyramidal symptoms treated with anticholinergic agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Adding anticholinergics to antipsychotics dramatically increases total anticholinergic burden, causing cognitive impairment, urinary retention, constipation, and delirium — especially in elderly patients. Validated scales (ACB, ARS) should quantify total burden. Preferred interventions include antipsychotic dose reduction, switch to lower-EPS-risk agents (quetiapine, clozapine), or use of amantadine which has lower anticholinergic burden.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El bloqueo D2 extrapiramidal combinado con anticolinérgicos incrementa drásticamente la carga anticolinérgica total → deterioro cognitivo, retención urinaria, estreñimiento y delirio, especialmente en ancianos; cuantificar la carga con escala ACB o ARS; preferir reducción de dosis, cambio a agentes de bajo riesgo EPS o amantadina

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic-induced hyperprolactinemia treated with dopamine agonists
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: This cascade creates a direct pharmacodynamic conflict: dopamine agonists may antagonize antipsychotic D2 blockade, potentially worsening psychosis. Adding cabergoline or bromocriptine without reviewing antipsychotic choice is clinically inappropriate. Switching to prolactin-sparing antipsychotics (quetiapine, aripiprazole, clozapine, olanzapine) is the recommended primary intervention.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Bloqueo D2 en la vía tuberoinfundibular → eliminación de la inhibición dopaminérgica → elevación de prolactina; los agonistas dopaminérgicos (cabergolina, bromocriptina) antagonizan el bloqueo D2 del antipsicótico y pueden exacerbar la psicosis; el cambio a antipsicóticos ahorradores de prolactina (quetiapina, aripiprazol, clozapina) es la intervención primaria recomendada

cascades.name_en: Antipsychotic/dopamine antagonist-induced akathisia treated with beta-blockers or benzodiazepines
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Akathisia is frequently misidentified as anxiety or psychotic agitation, prompting antipsychotic dose escalation which worsens the akathisia — a particularly dangerous feedback loop. Benzodiazepines increase sedation, respiratory depression, and fall risk. Propranolol is effective but contraindicated in asthma and heart block. Dose reduction of the causative agent should precede any pharmacological treatment of akathisia.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: La acatisia se confunde frecuentemente con ansiedad o agitación psicótica, provocando escalada de dosis del antipsicótico que empeora la acatisia (bucle de retroalimentación peligroso); las benzodiacepinas aumentan el riesgo de sedación, depresión respiratoria y caídas; el propranolol es eficaz pero está contraindicado en asma y bloqueo cardíaco; la reducción del fármaco causal debe preceder al tratamiento farmacológico

cascades.name_en: Lithium-induced hypothyroidism treated with levothyroxine
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Lithium inhibits thyroid hormone synthesis and release by blocking iodine transport and thyroglobulin proteolysis. Hypothyroidism occurs in up to 40% of long-term users, more commonly in women. While levothyroxine may be appropriate when lithium cannot be discontinued, this cascade occurs when thyroid function is not monitored regularly (recommended every 6 months) and the drug-induced nature of hypothyroidism is unrecognized.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El litio inhibe el transporte de yodo y la proteólisis de tiroglobulina → hipotiroidismo en hasta el 40% de los usuarios a largo plazo, más frecuente en mujeres; la monitorización tiroidea cada 6 meses es la práctica recomendada; la cascada ocurre cuando la naturaleza inducida por el fármaco del hipotiroidismo no se reconoce

cascades.name_en: Lithium-induced nephrogenic diabetes insipidus treated with amiloride or other agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Lithium accumulates in collecting duct principal cells via ENaC channels, impairing aquaporin-2 expression and water reabsorption. Amiloride blocks ENaC, reducing lithium entry — mechanistically rational but adds a drug. Indomethacin significantly reduces renal lithium clearance, increasing lithium toxicity risk. This cascade requires meticulous monitoring of lithium levels, renal function, and electrolytes.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El litio se acumula en las células principales del conducto colector vía canales ENaC → deterioro de la expresión de acuaporina-2 y la reabsorción de agua; la amilorida bloquea ENaC y reduce la entrada de litio (mecanísticamente racional pero añade un fármaco); la indometacina reduce significativamente el aclaramiento renal de litio aumentando el riesgo de toxicidad

cascades.name_en: Valproate-induced postural tremor treated with beta-blockers
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Valproate-induced tremor is dose-dependent and resolves with dose reduction in most cases. Adding propranolol introduces cardiovascular and pulmonary risks; primidone may cause significant pharmacokinetic interactions with valproate and add sedation. The appropriate primary intervention is valproate dose reduction or switch to extended-release formulation, not addition of a new drug.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El temblor inducido por valproato es dependiente de dosis y se resuelve con reducción de dosis en la mayoría de casos; añadir propranolol introduce riesgos cardiovasculares y pulmonares; la primidona puede causar interacciones farmacocinéticas significativas con el valproato y añade sedación; la reducción de dosis o el cambio a formulación retardada es la intervención primaria apropiada

cascades.name_en: SSRI/SNRI-induced bruxism treated with benzodiazepines or buspirone
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonin-mediated inhibition of dopaminergic pathways disrupts motor control during sleep, causing bruxism. Clonazepam increases fall risk, sedation, and carries dependency risk particularly in elderly patients. Buspirone (5-HT1A partial agonist) has some evidence for reversing SSRI-induced bruxism and a more favorable risk profile. Dose reduction of the causative SSRI/SNRI should be considered as the primary intervention.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: La inhibición serotonérgica de vías dopaminérgicas altera el control motor durante el sueño → bruxismo; el clonazepam aumenta el riesgo de caídas, sedación y dependencia, especialmente en ancianos; la buspirona (agonista parcial 5-HT1A) tiene evidencia para revertir el bruxismo por ISRS con mejor perfil de riesgo; considerar reducción de dosis del ISRS/IRSN como intervención primaria

cascades.name_en: Cholinesterase inhibitor-induced nausea and vomiting treated with antiemetics
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Cholinesterase inhibitor GI effects are dose-dependent and cholinergically mediated. CRITICAL CDS STRATIFICATION: (1) metoclopramide and prochlorperazine are centrally-acting D2 antagonists — they directly oppose the mechanism of AChEIs, worsen cognitive function in dementia patients, and increase extrapyramidal symptoms; these represent a CONTRAINDICATED cascade combination. (2) Domperidone (peripheral D2 antagonist) and ondansetron (5-HT3 antagonist) are relatively safer antiemetic options and should trigger a REVIEW-level alert rather than a contraindication. Preferred management for AChEI-induced nausea: slow titration, dose reduction, or switch to transdermal rivastigmine.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Efectos GI colinérgicamente mediados y dependientes de dosis; ESTRATIFICACIÓN CRÍTICA: metoclopramida y proclorperazina (antagonistas D2 centrales) ESTÁN CONTRAINDICADAS, ya que se oponen directamente al mecanismo de los IACEs y empeoran la función cognitiva; domperidona (antagonista D2 periférico) y ondansetrón (antagonista 5-HT3) son alternativas más seguras; manejo preferente: titulación lenta o cambio a rivastigmina transdérmica

cascades.name_es: Levodopa → Náuseas → Antiemético antagonista D2 central
cascades.name_en: Levodopa-induced nausea treated with centrally-acting dopamine antagonist antiemetics
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Centrally-acting dopamine antagonist antiemetics (metoclopramide, prochlorperazine, chlorpromazine, promethazine, haloperidol) are absolutely contraindicated in Parkinson disease. These agents block nigrostriatal D2 receptors and cause severe motor deterioration, potentially irreversible worsening of parkinsonism, and acute dystonic reactions. This cascade is particularly dangerous because Parkinson disease may be undiagnosed, undertreated, or unknown to the prescribing clinician (e.g. prescriber outside neurology). SAFE ALTERNATIVES for nausea in Parkinson patients: domperidone (peripheral D2 antagonist, limited CNS penetration; monitor QTc) or ondansetron (5-HT3 antagonist, no D2 activity). A structured medication history is mandatory before prescribing any antiemetic to a patient with Parkinson disease or on levodopa.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los antieméticos antagonistas D2 centrales (metoclopramida, proclorperazina, clorpromazina, haloperidol) están ABSOLUTAMENTE CONTRAINDICADOS en la enfermedad de Parkinson; bloquean receptores D2 nigroestriados causando deterioro motor grave y potencialmente irreversible; alternativas seguras: domperidona (antagonista D2 periférico, monitorizar QTc) u ondansetrón (antagonista 5-HT3, sin actividad D2)

cascades.name_en: NSAID-induced hypertension treated with antihypertensives
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: NSAIDs inhibit renal prostaglandin synthesis (PGE2, PGI2), causing sodium and water retention and directly blunting the effect of diuretics, ACE inhibitors, and ARBs. Adding antihypertensives without removing the NSAID is highly prevalent and futile as long as the NSAID continues. COX-2 selective inhibitors carry equivalent or greater cardiovascular risk. Paracetamol or topical NSAIDs are safer alternatives for pain.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los AINE inhiben la síntesis de prostaglandinas renales (PGE2, PGI2) → retención de sodio y agua + antagonismo directo de diuréticos, IECA y ARA-II; añadir antihipertensivos sin retirar el AINE es altamente prevalente y de escasa utilidad mientras el AINE continúa; el paracetamol o los AINE tópicos son alternativas analgésicas más seguras

cascades.name_en: NSAID-induced peripheral edema treated with diuretics
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: NSAIDs promote sodium and water retention via renal prostaglandin inhibition. Diuretics are largely ineffective while the NSAID is continued. The triple whammy combination (NSAID + diuretic + ACE inhibitor or ARB) is a well-documented cause of acute kidney injury in outpatient and hospital settings, particularly in elderly or volume-depleted patients. NSAID withdrawal should be the primary intervention.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los AINE promueven retención de sodio y agua vía inhibición de prostaglandinas renales; los diuréticos son ineficaces mientras el AINE continúa; la triple combinación (AINE + diurético + IECA/ARA-II) es causa bien documentada de lesión renal aguda en pacientes ancianos o con depleción de volumen; la retirada del AINE debe ser la intervención primaria

cascades.name_en: Systemic corticosteroid-induced hyperglycemia treated with antidiabetic agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Corticosteroids elevate postprandial glucose predominantly via hepatic gluconeogenesis stimulation and peripheral insulin resistance; fasting glucose may be minimally affected. CONTEXT IS ESSENTIAL: in patients on long-term or high-dose steroids (>7.5 mg prednisolone equivalent/day), antidiabetic treatment is clinically appropriate and necessary — this is not a cascade. The cascade occurs when: (a) short-course steroids cause transient hyperglycemia leading to initiation of antidiabetics that persist after steroid discontinuation, or (b) the postprandial-predominant pattern is not recognized and fasting-targeted basal insulin is the only intervention. NPH insulin timed with the steroid dose, or premixed insulin, is more mechanistically appropriate. Antidiabetic treatment must be reviewed with each steroid dose change or taper.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los corticosteroides estimulan la gluconeogénesis hepática y aumentan la resistencia insulínica periférica → hiperglucemia predominantemente postprandial con glucemia basal poco afectada; la cascada ocurre cuando el tratamiento antidiabético persiste tras retirar el esteroide de corta duración, o cuando no se reconoce el patrón postprandial; la insulina NPH sincronizada con la dosis del esteroide es más apropiada mecanísticamente

cascades.name_en: Systemic corticosteroid-induced hypertension treated with antihypertensives
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Corticosteroids exert mineralocorticoid-like effects causing sodium retention and volume expansion; mineralocorticoid potency varies across agents (fludrocortisone > prednisolone > dexamethasone). Spironolactone may partially counteract mineralocorticoid effects but carries hyperkalemia risk with RAAS inhibitors. Steroid dose minimization and the possibility of steroid-sparing disease-modifying therapy should be part of the therapeutic strategy.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los corticosteroides ejercen efectos mineralocorticoides → retención de sodio y expansión de volumen; potencia mineralocorticoide variable entre agentes (fludrocortisona > prednisolona > dexametasona); la espironolactona puede contrarrestar parcialmente los efectos mineralocorticoides; la minimización de la dosis de esteroides y la terapia inmunomoduladora ahorradora de esteroides deben incluirse en la estrategia terapéutica

cascades.name_es: Bisfosfonato oral → Irritación GI alta → IBP
cascades.name_en: Oral bisphosphonate-induced upper GI irritation treated with proton pump inhibitors
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Oral bisphosphonates cause upper GI irritation and esophagitis via direct chemical contact: the tablet or residue adheres to the esophageal mucosa, causing localized chemical irritation, disruption of mucosal cell membrane integrity (amphiphilic properties at low pH), and mucosal inflammation. This is a contact-mediated effect independent of systemic absorption. Adding a PPI instead of reviewing administration technique (strict fasting, upright posture, 200 mL water, 30-60 minute pre-meal delay) or switching to IV zoledronic acid adds drug burden. Critically: long-term PPI use independently reduces bone mineral density (impaired calcium and magnesium absorption) and causes hypomagnesemia, compounding the indication for which bisphosphonate was prescribed.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los bisfosfonatos orales causan irritación del tracto GI alto por contacto químico directo con la mucosa esofágica (efecto independiente de la absorción sistémica); revisar la técnica de administración (ayunas, posición erguida, 200 mL agua, espera de 30-60 min) o cambiar a ácido zoledrónico IV es preferible a añadir un IBP; el IBP a largo plazo reduce independientemente la densidad mineral ósea

cascades.name_es: Antipsicótico 2.ª generación → Síndrome metabólico → Antidiabético/Agente de control de peso
cascades.name_en: Second-generation antipsychotic-induced metabolic syndrome treated with antidiabetic or weight-loss agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: SGA-induced metabolic syndrome involves H1 histaminergic and M3 muscarinic receptor antagonism combined with serotonergic and dopaminergic modulation of hypothalamic satiety pathways. Adding metabolic agents without reviewing antipsychotic choice perpetuates the root cause. Switch to metabolically neutral agents (aripiprazole, ziprasidone, lurasidone) should be formally evaluated before initiating additional pharmacotherapy. Note: GLP-1 receptor agonists (liraglutide, semaglutide) have emerging evidence for SGA-induced weight management but add injection burden and GI side effects.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Antagonismo H1 histaminérgico y M3 muscarínico combinado con modulación serotoninérgica y dopaminérgica de vías hipotalámicas de saciedad → ganancia de peso y resistencia insulínica; evaluar formalmente el cambio a agentes metabólicamente neutros (aripiprazol, ziprasidona, lurasidona) antes de iniciar tratamiento metabólico adicional; los agonistas de GLP-1 tienen evidencia emergente para el control de peso inducido por AAG
cascades.ade_en: metabolic syndrome / weight gain / insulin resistance
cascades.ade_es: Síndrome metabólico / ganancia de peso / resistencia insulínica

cascades.name_en: Gabapentinoid-induced peripheral edema treated with diuretics
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Gabapentinoid edema is caused by increased microvascular permeability rather than cardiac or volume-overload mechanisms; diuretics are largely ineffective and add electrolyte disturbance risk. Edema often responds to dose reduction or drug discontinuation. This cascade is increasingly relevant given the substantial rise in gabapentinoid prescribing for neuropathic pain, anxiety, and fibromyalgia.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Aumento de la permeabilidad microvascular (no sobrecarga de volumen ni mecanismo cardíaco); los diuréticos son en gran medida ineficaces y añaden riesgo de alteraciones electrolíticas; el edema suele responder a la reducción de dosis o suspensión del gabapentinoide; cascada cada vez más relevante dado el aumento sustancial de la prescripción de gabapentinoides

cascades.name_en: Proton pump inhibitor-induced hypomagnesemia treated with magnesium supplements
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Chronic PPI use (>1 year) impairs active transcellular magnesium absorption in the small intestine via TRPM6/TRPM7 channel downregulation. Oral supplements have limited bioavailability when the PPI is continued; IV replacement may be required acutely. Recurrent hypomagnesemia is a strong indicator for PPI deprescription review. Concurrent loop diuretic use compounds magnesium losses.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El uso crónico de IBP (>1 año) deteriora la absorción activa de magnesio en el intestino delgado por infraregulación de canales TRPM6/TRPM7; los suplementos orales tienen biodisponibilidad limitada mientras el IBP continúa; puede requerirse reposición IV aguda; la hipomagnesemia recurrente es indicador sólido para revisión de deprescripción del IBP; el uso concomitante de diurético de asa agrava sinérgicamente las pérdidas de magnesio

cascades.name_es: Antiemético antagonista D2 → Parkinsonismo inducido por fármaco → Levodopa
cascades.name_en: Dopamine antagonist antiemetic-induced parkinsonism misdiagnosed as Parkinson disease, treated with levodopa
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Drug-induced parkinsonism (DIP) caused by central D2 antagonists (metoclopramide, prochlorperazine, chlorpromazine, haloperidol) is clinically indistinguishable from idiopathic Parkinson disease and is frequently misdiagnosed, leading to initiation of levodopa or dopamine agonists. Initiating antiparkinsonian therapy without identifying and withdrawing the causative agent is serious; symptoms may require weeks to months to resolve after drug withdrawal. Note: domperidone is NOT included as a trigger — it is a peripheral D2 antagonist with limited CNS penetration at standard doses and substantially lower DIP risk than central D2 blockers; it should not co-trigger this cascade alert. DaT-SPECT can help differentiate DIP from idiopathic PD, but a complete medication history is the essential first step.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El parkinsonismo inducido por fármacos (PIF) causado por antagonistas D2 centrales (metoclopramida, proclorperazina, clorpromazina, haloperidol) es clínicamente indistinguible de la EP idiopática y frecuentemente diagnosticado de forma errónea, llevando a la instauración de levodopa sin retirar el agente causal; la domperidona NO se incluye como desencadenante (penetración CNS mínima a dosis estándar); la historia farmacológica completa es el primer paso esencial

cascades.name_en: Anticholinergic drug-induced constipation treated with laxatives
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Anticholinergics reduce colonic motility via M3 muscarinic receptor blockade throughout the GI tract. Adding laxatives without assessing total anticholinergic burden (ACB score, ARS) is a high-frequency cascade in elderly and polypharmacy patients. In severe cases, anticholinergic-induced ileus may mask acute abdominal pathology. Total anticholinergic load should be quantified and minimized.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Bloqueo de receptores muscarínicos M3 en todo el tracto GI → reducción de la motilidad colónica; añadir laxantes sin cuantificar la carga anticolinérgica total (puntuación ACB, ARS) es una cascada de alta frecuencia en pacientes polimedicados y ancianos; en casos graves, el íleo anticolinérgico puede enmascarar patología abdominal aguda

cascades.name_en: Mirabegron-induced hypertension treated with antihypertensives
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Mirabegron activates beta-3 adrenergic receptors on bladder detrusor muscle, causing relaxation and increasing bladder capacity. A clinically observed but modest increase in blood pressure (mean SBP +1-2 mmHg per FDA label) has been documented, particularly at higher doses. Mechanism note: vascular beta-3 receptor activation stimulates eNOS and causes vasodilation — not vasoconstriction. The hypertensive effect may involve cardiac beta-3 stimulation (increased inotropy/chronotropy) or other sympathomimetic mechanisms. Mirabegron is contraindicated in severe uncontrolled hypertension (SBP >180 mmHg or DBP >110 mmHg). Blood pressure monitoring at initiation is required; mirabegron discontinuation and alternative OAB treatment review is preferred over adding an antihypertensive. Vibegron has greater bladder selectivity.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: La activación de receptores beta-3 adrenérgicos en el detrusor vesical puede asociarse a un aumento leve de la TA (media +1-2 mmHg según ficha técnica); contraindicado en hipertensión grave no controlada (TAS >180 mmHg o TAD >110 mmHg); el vibegrón tiene mayor selectividad vesical; preferir la revisión y suspensión del mirabegrón frente a añadir un antihipertensivo

cascades.name_en: HIV protease inhibitor-induced dyslipidemia treated with statins
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Ritonavir-boosted protease inhibitors cause dyslipidemia via impaired lipid clearance and adipokine dysregulation. Critical DDI: ritonavir is a potent CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein inhibitor. Simvastatin and lovastatin are contraindicated (severe rhabdomyolysis risk). Preferred statins are pravastatin (not CYP3A4-metabolized) or pitavastatin. Rosuvastatin levels are increased by some PIs via OATP1B1. ART regimen review for switch to integrase-based, non-boosted regimens should precede statin initiation.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los IP potenciados con ritonavir deterioran el aclaramiento lipídico y la regulación de adipocinas; el ritonavir es potente inhibidor de CYP3A4 y P-gp: simvastatina y lovastatina CONTRAINDICADAS (grave riesgo de rabdomiólisis); preferir pravastatina (no metabolizada por CYP3A4) o pitavastatina; evaluar cambio a régimen basado en INSTI sin potenciación antes de iniciar estatina

cascades.name_es: TDF → Tubulopatía renal proximal → Suplementos minerales
cascades.name_en: Tenofovir disoproxil fumarate-induced renal tubular dysfunction treated with mineral supplements
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: TDF causes mitochondrial toxicity in renal proximal tubular cells, potentially leading to full Fanconi syndrome (phosphaturia, glycosuria, proteinuria, aminoaciduria). Supplementing phosphate and vitamin D without switching to tenofovir alafenamide (TAF) — which has substantially lower renal and bone toxicity due to intracellular prodrug activation — fails to address the mechanism and allows progressive renal and bone damage. Routine monitoring of eGFR, phosphate, urine protein/creatinine ratio, and bone density is essential.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El TDF causa toxicidad mitocondrial en células tubulares proximales → posible síndrome de Fanconi completo (fosfaturia, glucosuria, proteinuria, aminoaciduria); suplementar fosfato y vitamina D sin cambiar a TAF (activación intracelular del profármaco con toxicidad renal/ósea sustancialmente menor) no actúa sobre el mecanismo y permite el daño progresivo; monitorización de FGe, fosfato, cociente proteína/creatinina en orina y densidad ósea es esencial

cascades.name_en: Efavirenz-induced neuropsychiatric symptoms treated with antidepressants
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Efavirenz causes CNS adverse effects in up to 50% of patients partly via CYP2B6 slow-metabolizer pharmacogenetics, leading to elevated drug exposure. Adding an antidepressant without switching ART ignores the pharmacological cause and adds DDI risk (CYP2B6 interactions). Modern integrase strand transfer inhibitor-based regimens (dolutegravir, bictegravir) have substantially better CNS tolerability and should be preferred. CYP2B6 genotyping informs risk stratification.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El efavirenz causa efectos adversos CNS en hasta el 50% de los pacientes, parcialmente por la farmacogenética de CYP2B6 (metabolizadores lentos con mayor exposición al fármaco); añadir antidepresivos sin cambiar el TAR ignora la causa farmacológica y añade riesgo de DDI (interacciones CYP2B6); los regímenes basados en INSTI modernos (dolutegravir, bictegravir) tienen mejor tolerabilidad CNS y deben preferirse

cascades.name_es: TAR basado en IP → Resistencia insulínica → Antidiabético
cascades.name_en: Protease inhibitor-based antiretroviral therapy-induced insulin resistance treated with antidiabetic agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Older HIV protease inhibitors (especially indinavir) directly inhibit GLUT4 transporter function, acutely impairing insulin-stimulated peripheral glucose uptake. This is compounded by lipodystrophy and visceral fat accumulation. Newer integrase-based regimens have substantially lower metabolic effects. ART regimen optimization — switching away from boosted older PIs — should be formally evaluated before initiating antidiabetic pharmacotherapy.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los IP del VIH más antiguos (especialmente indinavir) inhiben directamente el transportador GLUT4 → deterioro agudo de la captación periférica de glucosa estimulada por insulina; compuesto por lipodistrofia y acumulación de grasa visceral; la optimización del TAR (cambio a regímenes basados en INSTI con efectos metabólicos sustancialmente menores) debe evaluarse formalmente antes de iniciar antidiabéticos

cascades.name_es: Inhibidor de calcineurina → Hipertensión → Antagonista del calcio
cascades.name_en: Calcineurin inhibitor-induced hypertension treated with calcium channel blockers
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Non-dihydropyridine CCBs (diltiazem, verapamil) significantly increase calcineurin inhibitor exposure via strong CYP3A4 inhibition, markedly raising nephrotoxicity and immunosuppression toxicity risk. Dihydropyridine CCBs (amlodipine) are preferred but modestly increase cyclosporine levels via CYP3A4 — therapeutic drug monitoring adjustment is required. This is a bidirectionally relevant pharmacokinetic cascade.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los BCC no dihidropiridínicos (diltiazem, verapamilo) aumentan significativamente la exposición al inhibidor de calcineurina por inhibición potente de CYP3A4, elevando marcadamente el riesgo de nefrotoxicidad y toxicidad por inmunosupresión; los BCC dihidropiridínicos (amlodipino) son la alternativa preferida aunque aumentan moderadamente los niveles de ciclosporina vía CYP3A4; se requiere monitorización de niveles del inmunosupresor

cascades.name_en: Tacrolimus-induced post-transplant diabetes mellitus treated with antidiabetic agents
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Tacrolimus impairs pancreatic beta-cell insulin secretion via calcineurin-dependent inhibition of NFAT transcription factors regulating insulin gene expression. PTDM is multifactorial (tacrolimus + concurrent corticosteroids). Tacrolimus minimization or conversion to cyclosporine may be considered in collaboration with transplant teams. Metformin is generally avoided early post-transplant due to renal impairment risk. This cascade requires multidisciplinary management.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El tacrolimus deteriora la secreción de insulina por las células beta pancreáticas mediante inhibición calcineurina-dependiente de los factores de transcripción NFAT que regulan la expresión del gen de la insulina; la DMPT es multifactorial (tacrolimus + corticosteroides concomitantes); la minimización de tacrolimus o la conversión a ciclosporina puede considerarse con el equipo de trasplante; la metformina se evita generalmente en el postrasplante precoz por riesgo de insuficiencia renal

cascades.name_en: Cyclosporine-induced hyperuricemia and gout treated with colchicine or allopurinol
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Cyclosporine reduces renal urate excretion via OAT transporter inhibition. Critical DDI: colchicine toxicity (rhabdomyolysis, neuromyopathy, pancytopenia) is markedly increased by cyclosporine via combined P-glycoprotein and CYP3A4 inhibition — colchicine dose must be substantially reduced and duration limited. Allopurinol-azathioprine interaction (xanthine oxidase inhibition with TPMT pathway shift) is separately lethal in transplant patients. NSAIDs are contraindicated due to nephrotoxicity.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: La ciclosporina reduce la excreción renal de urato por inhibición de transportadores OAT; DDI CRÍTICA: la toxicidad de la colchicina (rabdomiólisis, neuromiopatía, pancitopenia) aumenta drásticamente por la inhibición combinada de P-gp y CYP3A4 por ciclosporina — la dosis debe reducirse sustancialmente y la duración limitarse; la interacción alopurinol-azatioprina (inhibición de xantina oxidasa con desvío hacia TPMT) puede ser letal en pacientes trasplantados; los AINE están contraindicados por nefrotoxicidad

cascades.name_es: Diurético de asa → Hipomagnesemia → Suplemento de magnesio
cascades.name_en: Loop diuretic-induced hypomagnesemia treated with magnesium supplements
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Loop diuretics inhibit NKCC2 transporter in the thick ascending limb of Henle, abolishing the electrochemical gradient required for passive paracellular magnesium reabsorption. Magnesium depletion potentiates hypokalemia and ventricular arrhythmias. Concurrent PPI use compounds magnesium loss synergistically. Loop diuretic dose minimization and dietary magnesium intake should be optimized alongside supplementation; refractory hypokalemia unresponsive to potassium supplementation should prompt magnesium level check.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los diuréticos de asa inhiben el transportador NKCC2 en el asa de Henle ascendente gruesa → abolición del gradiente electroquímico para la reabsorción paracelular pasiva de magnesio; la depleción de magnesio potencia la hipopotasemia y las arritmias ventriculares; el uso concomitante de IBP agrava sinérgicamente las pérdidas de magnesio; optimizar la dosis del diurético de asa y la ingesta dietética de magnesio junto con la suplementación

cascades.name_es: Diurético de asa → Hipopotasemia → Suplemento de K⁺/Diurético ahorrador de K⁺
cascades.name_en: Loop diuretic-induced hypokalemia treated with potassium supplements or potassium-sparing diuretics
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Loop diuretics cause potassium wasting via increased distal nephron sodium delivery driving K+ secretion. In heart failure patients on concurrent ACE inhibitors or ARBs, added potassium supplementation creates significant hyperkalemia risk — a combination that requires careful electrolyte monitoring. Spironolactone in heart failure with reduced ejection fraction may address both hypokalemia and mortality benefit simultaneously, making it a rational substitution rather than addition.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Los diuréticos de asa aumentan el aporte de sodio al nefrón distal → mayor secreción de K⁺; en pacientes con insuficiencia cardíaca en tratamiento concomitante con IECA/ARA-II, el suplemento de K⁺ añadido crea riesgo significativo de hiperpotasemia que requiere monitorización cuidadosa del ionograma; la espironolactona en la ICFEr puede abordar simultáneamente la hipopotasemia y ofrecer beneficio en mortalidad

cascades.name_en: Fluoroquinolone-induced tendinopathy treated with NSAIDs
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Fluoroquinolones inhibit tenocyte proliferation and matrix metalloproteinase homeostasis, impairing tendon structural integrity via mechanisms independent of inflammation. NSAIDs treat symptom but do not address structural damage and may mask warning symptoms, increasing tendon rupture risk. Fluoroquinolone discontinuation and strict physical activity restriction are mandatory. Risk is amplified in elderly patients, those receiving concurrent corticosteroids, and solid organ transplant recipients.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Las fluoroquinolonas inhiben la proliferación de tenocitos y la homeostasis de metaloproteasas de la matriz → daño estructural tendinoso independiente de la inflamación; los AINE tratan el síntoma pero no actúan sobre el daño estructural y pueden enmascarar señales de alarma, aumentando el riesgo de rotura tendinosa; la suspensión de la fluoroquinolona y la restricción estricta de la actividad física son obligatorias; el riesgo se amplifica en ancianos, pacientes con corticosteroides concomitantes y receptores de trasplante de órganos sólidos

cascades.name_es: ISRS/IRSN → Disfunción sexual → Inhibidor de PDE5
cascades.name_en: SSRI/SNRI-induced sexual dysfunction treated with PDE5 inhibitors
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Serotonin excess suppresses dopaminergic and noradrenergic pathways involved in sexual arousal. PDE5 inhibitors address erectile dysfunction only; libido reduction, anorgasmia, and delayed ejaculation are not improved. Interaction risk with nitrates is relevant in the cardiovascular patient population. Alternatives with lower sexual dysfunction burden include bupropion, mirtazapine, or dose reduction; a structured SSRI dose review should precede PDE5 inhibitor prescribing.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: El exceso de serotonina suprime las vías dopaminérgicas y noradrenérgicas implicadas en la excitación sexual; los inhibidores de PDE5 solo abordan la disfunción eréctil; la reducción de la libido, la anorgasmia y la eyaculación retardada no mejoran; el riesgo de interacción con nitratos es relevante en la población cardiovascular; alternativas con menor carga en la función sexual: bupropión, mirtazapina o reducción de dosis del ISRS/IRSN

cascades.name_es: Antipsicótico con alta afinidad alfa-1 → Hipotensión ortostática → Vasopresor
cascades.name_en: High-anticholinergic antipsychotic-induced orthostatic hypotension treated with vasopressors
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Antipsychotics with high alpha-1 adrenergic receptor affinity (clozapine, chlorpromazine, quetiapine, thioridazine) cause pronounced vasodilation and orthostatic hypotension. Risperidone has intermediate alpha-1 activity. NOTE: haloperidol has been removed from the trigger list — it has substantially lower alpha-1 affinity (Ki ~40 nM) than clozapine or chlorpromazine; orthostatic hypotension is uncommon with haloperidol at standard doses and its inclusion would generate high false-positive rates. Adding midodrine or fludrocortisone without reviewing antipsychotic agent and dose significantly increases cardiovascular burden and supine hypertension risk. Dose reduction and slow titration protocols should precede vasopressor addition.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Alta afinidad por el receptor alfa-1 adrenérgico (clozapina, clorpromazina, quetiapina, tioridazina) → vasodilatación sistémica pronunciada y caída postural de TA; el haloperidol ha sido excluido del desencadenante (menor afinidad alfa-1, hipotensión ortostática infrecuente a dosis estándar); añadir midodrina o fludrocortisona sin revisar el agente y la dosis del antipsicótico aumenta la carga cardiovascular y el riesgo de hipertensión supina; preferir titulación lenta y protocolos de reducción de dosis

cascades.name_en: Clozapine-induced sialorrhea treated with systemic anticholinergics
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Clozapine-induced sialorrhea is paradoxical: despite broad anticholinergic effects, clozapine stimulates salivary secretion via partial M4 muscarinic agonism and alpha-2 adrenergic antagonism (reducing sympathetic inhibition of salivary glands). CRITICAL DISTINCTION in treatment_drug CNS penetration: hyoscine butylbromide and glycopyrronium are QUATERNARY AMMONIUM compounds (QACs) with minimal oral bioavailability and negligible CNS penetration — their contribution to central anticholinergic burden is minimal; these are acceptable for topical/local oral use. Oxybutynin (tertiary amine) readily crosses the BBB and substantially increases central anticholinergic burden, causing cognitive impairment and delirium — it is the most problematic agent in this list for schizophrenia patients. Preferred approach: topical sublingual anticholinergics (ipratropium, atropine ophthalmic drops) to minimize systemic and CNS exposure.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: Sialorrea paradójica por agonismo parcial M4 y antagonismo alfa-2 → estimulación de las glándulas salivales a pesar de los efectos anticolinérgicos generales de la clozapina; DISTINCIÓN CRÍTICA: los compuestos de amonio cuaternario (glicopirronio, butilescopolamina) tienen biodisponibilidad oral mínima y penetración CNS negligible, añadiendo mínima carga anticolinérgica central; la oxibutinina (amina terciaria) cruza fácilmente la BHE y es el agente más problemático de esta lista en pacientes con esquizofrenia; preferir anticolinérgicos sublinguales tópicos (ipratropio, colirio de atropina)

cascades.name_en: Clozapine-induced constipation treated with laxatives
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Clozapine has the highest anticholinergic burden of any antipsychotic and causes severe, potentially life-threatening constipation including paralytic ileus and intestinal obstruction (reported mortality). Reactive treatment of established constipation with laxatives is a cascade that occurs because bowel monitoring protocols are often absent. Active, protocol-driven prophylactic laxative prescription and regular bowel function assessment should be standard practice during clozapine treatment. Late recognition may require surgical intervention.
cascades.ade_mechanism_es: La clozapina posee la mayor carga anticolinérgica de todos los antipsicóticos y causa estreñimiento grave, potencialmente con riesgo vital (íleo paralítico, obstrucción intestinal con mortalidad documentada); el tratamiento reactivo del estreñimiento establecido con laxantes es una cascada que ocurre porque los protocolos de vigilancia intestinal frecuentemente están ausentes; la prescripción profiláctica protocolizada de laxantes y la evaluación regular de la función intestinal deben ser práctica estándar durante el tratamiento con clozapina

cascades.name_en: Activating antidepressant-induced insomnia treated with sedative-hypnotics
cascades.ade_mechanism_en: Activating antidepressants (fluoxetine, venlafaxine, bupropion, desvenlafaxine) frequently cause insomnia via noradrenergic and serotonergic activation, particularly when dosed in the evening. Duloxetine has a mixed profile: noradrenergic activation may cause insomnia in some patients but sedation in others — retained as trigger with this caveat. CORRECTIONS: 'trazodone low-dose' and 'mirtazapine low-dose' removed from treatment_drug: both are antidepressants with distinct mechanisms; prescribing them as 'sleep aids' while the patient is on an SSRI/SNRI creates two-antidepressant polypharmacy, adds serotonergic burden, and introduces separate pharmacological complexity not appropriate for cascade detection classification.
